problem_addressed: Current machine learning potentials struggle with uncertainty quantification, limiting their reliability for active learning, calibration, and out-of-distribution detection.
core_claim: Bayesian E(3)-equivariant MLPs with iterative restratification and joint energy-force uncertainty modeling achieve competitive accuracy while enabling reliable uncertainty quantification.
key_contributions: Development of Bayesian E(3)-equivariant interatomic potential with iterative restratification of many-body message passing
Introduction of joint energy-force negative log-likelihood (NLLJEF) loss function for simultaneous energy and force uncertainty modeling
novelty: Combines Bayesian uncertainty quantification with E(3)-equivariant architecture and introduces NLLJEF loss to model joint energy-force uncertainties, closing performance gap between Bayesian and deterministic approaches
method_summary: Implements Bayesian neural networks with E(3)-equivariant message passing, using iterative restratification and NLLJEF loss to quantify uncertainties in both energies and forces simultaneously.
method_components: Joint energy-force negative log-likelihood (NLLJEF) loss function
Uncertainty quantification via deep ensembles, SWAG, IVON, and Laplace approximation
key_innovation: NLLJEF loss function enables simultaneous optimization of energy and force uncertainties, reducing energy errors by 3-4× and force errors by 2-3× compared to energy-only uncertainty modeling while maintaining predictive accuracy
assumptions: Uncertainty quantification is essential for reliable MLPs in active learning and OOD detection
E(3)-equivariance is necessary for accurate interatomic potential modeling
Joint energy-force uncertainty modeling provides complementary information
theoretical_grounding: No explicit proofs or bounds provided; relies on empirical validation across multiple datasets and uncertainty quantification metrics
hyperparameters: λE and λF for energy/force loss weighting; 2-head MVE for energy-only, 8-head MVE for joint energy-force; softplus/exponential activation for variance
ablations: Iterative restratification of many-body features vs. fixed-order features in MACE; comparison of energy decomposition schemes (atomic vs. pairwise vs. restratified)
title: Bayesian E(3)-Equivariant Interatomic Potential with Iterative Restratification of Many-body Message Passing
